title: tests: skip CRIU tests on overlay
body:
CRIU doesn't work on overlay and the CI is failing these tests when
running in a container.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Cool, I was also hitting this and it was not clear why it fails. So this sounds good from my point of view. There are however still some CI failures. So this probably needs to be added for all checkpoint test cases.

My other idea was, as CRIU is pulled in as a weak dependency from runc (I think) to exclude it from the Dockerfile.Fedora.
Is there a way to get the dump.log files from the CRIU test failures? On my RHEL7 test system I can run podman in podman and I do not get errors.

I was finally able to reproduce this and it does not seem to be overlayfs related as far as I can tell.

CRIU tries to lock the network during checkpointing using iptables and I see the following error in the CRIU log file:
```
(00.132695) Running network-lock scripts
(00.132724) 	RPC
ip6tables-restore v1.6.2: ip6tables-restore: unable to initialize table 'filter'

Error occurred at line: 1
Try `ip6tables-restore -h' or 'ip6tables-restore --help' for more information.
(00.167922) Error (criu/util.c:816): exited, status=2
(00.168277) Error (criu/net.c:2611): Locking network failed: iptables-restore returned -1. This may be connected to disabled CONFIG_NETFILTER_XT_MARK kernel build config option.
(00.168430) Unlock network
(00.168467) Running network-unlock scripts
(00.168496) 	RPC
ip6tables-restore v1.6.2: ip6tables-restore: unable to initialize table 'filter'

Error occurred at line: 1
Try `ip6tables-restore -h' or 'ip6tables-restore --help' for more information.
(00.194584) Error (criu/util.c:816): exited, status=2
(00.194753) Unfreezing tasks into 1
(00.194780) 	Unseizing 7383 into 1
(00.194903) Error (criu/cr-dump.c:1732): Dumping FAILED.
```

CRIU does basically the following:
```
echo -e "*filter\n:CRIU - [0:0]\n-I INPUT -j CRIU\n-A CRIU -m mark --mark 42 -j ACCEPT\nCOMMIT\n" | ip6tables-restore -w 1 --noflush
```

Just by loading the correct ip6tables nat module I was able to fix it. I am trying it right now to see if this fixes the test cases in CI.
thanks for tracking this down.  Do you have a open PR?

I am currently (ab)using my WIP PR https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1848, I pushed a test commit with two modprobe lines. Not sure if modprobe actually works in `.papr_prepare.sh`. Let's see what the test result says and if it works I can open a new PR or you can use it in this PR.
closing in favor of a patch in: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1848
